#--------------------
# Description_Notes_and_Keywords
#   Description: Temperature and elevation reconstructions for 7 Antarctic ice core locations (WAIS Divide, EPICA Dronning Maud Land, EPICA Dome C, TALDICE (Talos Dome), Siple Dome, SPICEcore (South Pole), and Dome Fuji) for the past 40,000 years, based on Antarctic ice core borehole temperature and d15N. Provided Keywords: Antarctica, Surface temperature, Surface accumulation, Last Glacial Maximum
#--------------------
# Publication
#   Authors: Buizert, Christo; Fudge, T.J.; Roberts, William H. G.; Steig, Eric J.; Sherriff-Tadano, Sam; Ritz, Catherine; Lefebvre, Eric; Edwards, Jon; Kawamura, Kenji; Oyabu, Ikumi; Motoyama, Hideaki; Kahle, Emma C.; Jones, Tyler R.; Abe-Ouchi, Ayako; Obase, Takashi; Martin, Carlos; Corr, Hugh; Severinghaus, Jeffrey P.; Beaudette, Ross; Epifanio, Jenna; Brook, Edward J.; Martin, Kaden; Chappellaz, Jérôme; Aoki, Shuji; Nakazawa, Takakiyo; Sowers, Todd A.; Alley, Richard; Ahn, Jinho; Sigl, Michael; Severi, Mirko; Dunbar, Nelia W.; Svensson, Anders; Fegyveresi, John; He, Chengfei; Liu, Zhengyu; Zhu, Jiang; Otto-Bliesner, Bette; Lipenkov, Vladimir Y.; Kameda, Takao; Schwander, Jakob
#   Journal_Name: Science
#   Published_Title: Antarctic surface temperature and elevation during the Last Glacial Maximum
#   Published_Date_or_Year: 2021
#   Volume: 372
#   Pages: 1097-1101
#   Issue: 6546
#   Report_Number: 
#   DOI: 10.1126/science.abd2897
#   Full_Citation:
#   Abstract: Water-stable isotopes in polar ice cores are a widely used temperature proxy in paleoclimate reconstruction, yet calibration remains challenging in East Antarctica. Here, we reconstruct the magnitude and spatial pattern of Last Glacial Maximum surface cooling in Antarctica using borehole thermometry and firn properties in seven ice cores. West Antarctic sites cooled ~10°C relative to the preindustrial period. East Antarctic sites show a range from ~4° to ~7°C cooling, which is consistent with the results of global climate models when the effects of topographic changes indicated with ice core air-content data are included, but less than those indicated with the use of water-stable isotopes calibrated against modern spatial gradients. An altered Antarctic temperature inversion during the glacial reconciles our estimates with water-isotope observations.
#--------------------

# Variables
# PaST_Thesaurus_Download_Resource: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/paleo-search/skos/past-thesaurus.rdf
#   PaST_Thesaurus_Download_Description: Paleoenvironmental Standard Terms (PaST) Thesaurus terms, definitions, and relationships in SKOS format.
#
# Data variables follow that are preceded by '##' in columns one and two.
# Variables list, one per line, shortname-tab-var components: what, material, error, units, seasonality, data type, detail, method, C or N for Character or Numeric data)
#
## age_yrBP1950	ice age,,,calendar year before present,,ice cores,,,N,WD2014 chronology; ice age relative to 1950
## A_EDC	accumulation rate,bulk ice,,meter per year,,ice cores,,inverse model,N,Accumulation rate at EPICA Dome C; meter per annum ice equivalent
## A_EDC_lower	accumulation rate,bulk ice,95% confidence interval lower bound,meter per year,,ice cores,,inverse model,N,Accumulation rate at EPICA Dome C; meter per annum ice equivalent
## A_EDC_upper	accumulation rate,bulk ice,95% confidence interval upper bound,meter per year,,ice cores,,inverse model,N,Accumulation rate at EPICA Dome C; meter per annum ice equivalent
